homePage.heroText: Adventure awaits here. No matter who you are or where you come from, there is an adventure waiting for you.
homePage.aboutDesc1: Hässelby Strands Sjöscoutkår (HSS) is a traditional, non-profit youth organization focusing on scouting and boating.
homePage.aboutDesc2: We are religiously and politically independent, and a member of the Swedish Scout Association, which with approximately 65,000 members is one of Sweden's largest youth organizations.

cardAventyrare.desc: This is where sailing begins in earnest, and we head out into the archipelago during the summer. When we aren't sailing, we practice navigation and also do traditional land-based scouting activities. In the winter, when it is at its coldest, we go on a proper winter hike where we camp in tents.
cardAventyrare.title: Adventurers

cardFamily.desc: In 'Småttarne', children and adults discover scouting together through play, nature experiences, and simpler outdoor adventures. Here, families can try out scouting at a relaxed pace with a focus on community and the joy of discovery.
cardFamily.title: Family Scouting

cardSparare.desc: At 8 years of age you can start in HSS, i.e., when you start 2nd grade. You then become a Trackers Scout and a member of the Sjöhumlorna/Trackers unit. (Trackers scouts were previously called 'miniorscouter')
cardSparare.title: Trackers

cardUpptackare.desc: At Kaparna, the difficulty level of the tasks increases. The scouts learn to sail Optimist dinghies and also try sailing 2-Krona and keelboats. A fully trained Discoverer scout can, among other things, handle a knife, build a fire, and extinguish small fires.
cardUpptackare.title: Discoverers

cardUtmanare.desc: When you graduate from the Adventurer scouts, you form a team together with your peers. You then work towards learning new things and taking on tougher challenges to strengthen team cohesion.
cardUtmanare.title: Challengers

homePage.safetyCardText1: The Scouts should be a safe place where you can explore and be challenged on your own terms. That is why we work with
homePage.safetyCardText3: The goal is for Scouting to be free from harassment and abuse in all forms.
homePage.safetyAdviceSupport: Advice and support - when something happens

bliScoutPage.heroText: If you or your child are thinking about joining the scouts, below is information on what can be expected of you or your child.
bliScoutPage.startStepDesc1: Submit your interest by clicking the link and filling out the details for you or your child. This will be sent directly to our membership registry.
bliScoutPage.startStepDesc2: We will get in touch shortly. Fields marked with * are mandatory.
bliScoutPage.startStepDesc3: If there is a waiting list, a trial day might pop up. Keep an eye out!
bliScoutPage.howItWorksDesc1: We have meetings once a week during the school term. The meetings are about 2 hours long and vary in content. In addition to the weekly meetings, we regularly organize hikes (overnight stays) and camps during weekends and holidays. The activities are divided into units based on age. Each unit has its own leaders and its own program tailored to the age group.
bliScoutPage.howItWorksDesc2: In addition to the weekly meetings, we regularly organize hikes (overnight stays) and camps during weekends and holidays. The activities are divided into units based on age. Each unit has its own leaders and its own program tailored to the age group.
bliScoutPage.howItWorksDesc3: The activities are divided into units based on age. Each unit has its own leaders and its own program tailored to the age group.
bliScoutPage.needToKnowDesc1: As a parent, you are always welcome to participate in our activities. We appreciate parental involvement, and many of our activities require support from parents.
bliScoutPage.needToKnowDesc2: All of our leaders are trained in 'Safe Meetings' and have undergone background checks. We work actively to create a safe and inclusive environment for all children and youth.
bliScoutPage.needToKnowDesc3: Communication is mainly done via email. You will receive information on how to access these channels after registration.
bliScoutPage.clothingDesc1: All scouts have a scout shirt purchased via the Scoutshop. The shirt is used on official occasions and features our badges and insignia.
bliScoutPage.clothingDesc2: Since we are Sea Scouts, life jackets are mandatory for all water activities. The troupe provides life jackets, but you are welcome to bring your own.
bliScoutPage.clothingDesc3: For hikes and camps, packing lists are sent out in advance. Basic outdoor clothing and a sleeping bag are good to have.
bliScoutPage.questionsSubTitle1: At what age can you start?
bliScoutPage.questionsSubTitle3: Do you need to know how to swim?
bliScoutPage.questionsSubTitle4: How do I know if my child got a spot?
bliScoutPage.questionsDesc1: You can start as a scout with us at 8 years of age, when you are in 2nd grade.
bliScoutPage.questionsDesc2: There is an annual membership fee that covers core activities. Extra costs apply for camps and larger activities. Contact us for current prices.
bliScoutPage.questionsDesc3: It is good if the child can swim, but it is not a requirement. We always use life jackets during water activities.
bliScoutPage.questionsDesc4: We will contact you via email or phone as soon as a spot becomes available. If demand is high, there may be a waiting list.

avdelningarPage.heroText: The activities are divided by age. Each unit has its own program and its own challenges.

trackers.title: Trackers
trackers.desc1: At 8 years of age you can start in HSS, i.e., when you start 2nd grade.
trackers.desc2: You then become a Trackers Scout and a member of the Sjöhumlorna unit.
trackers.whatWeDo1: What the unit does: Tracker scouting is about learning the basics of scouting through play and adventure.
trackers.whatWeDo2: Children get to try different activities both indoors and outdoors, learn about nature, and develop teamwork skills.
trackers.meeting: Meeting times: We have meetings every week during the school term. In addition to the weekly meetings, we have regular hikes (overnight stays) and longer camps during holidays.
trackers.contact: Leaders/contact: The unit is led by trained leaders. Contact us via info@hss-scout.org for more information.

discoverers.title: Discoverers
discoverers.desc1: At Kaparna, the difficulty level of the tasks increases. The scouts learn to sail Optimist dinghies and try sailing 2-Krona and keelboats.
discoverers.whatWeDo1: What the unit does: Discoverer scouting introduces more advanced skills. The children learn sailing, navigation, knots, nature studies, and teamwork in patrols.
discoverers.skills: Skills: A fully trained Discoverer scout can, among other things, handle a knife, build a fire and extinguish small fires, sail an Optimist by themselves, as well as basic first aid and maritime safety.
discoverers.meeting: Meeting times: Weekly meetings during the school term as well as hikes and camps. During the summer, we often sail in the archipelago.
discoverers.contact: Leaders/contact: Experienced leaders with sailing competence lead the activities. Contact info@hss-scout.org.

adventurers.title: Adventurers
adventurers.desc1: This is where sailing begins in earnest, and we head out into the archipelago during the summer. When we aren't sailing, we practice navigation and also do traditional land-based scouting activities.
adventurers.whatWeDo1: What the unit does: Adventurer scouts focus on more advanced sailing, navigation, and leadership. The patrols are given greater responsibility and work independently on their projects.
adventurers.activities: Summer sailing in the archipelago, navigation, land scouting activities, winter hikes in tents, and the traditional ice-hole swim.
adventurers.meeting: Meeting times: Weekly meetings and regular hikes. During the summer, we have longer sailing weeks.
adventurers.contact: Leaders/contact: The patrol works independently with support from experienced leaders. Contact info@hss-scout.org

challengers.title: Challengers
challengers.desc1: When you graduate from the Adventurer scouts, you form a team together with your peers. You then work towards learning new things and taking on tougher challenges.
challengers.whatWeDo1: What the unit does: Challenger scouting is the most advanced level where the youth plan and carry out their activities themselves. They work on personal development and leadership.
challengers.whatWeDo2: Leadership: Challengers start serving as leaders for the younger units and develop their leadership skills. They learn to plan activities and take responsibility for operations.
challengers.meeting: Meeting times: Flexible meetings based on the team's needs. Focus is on larger projects and leadership responsibility.
challengers.contact: Leaders/contact: The teams work independently. For more information, contact info@hss-scout.org

family.title: Family Scouting
family.desc1: In Family Scouting, children and adults discover scouting together through play, nature experiences, and simpler outdoor adventures.
family.desc2: Here, families can try out scouting at a relaxed pace with a focus on community and the joy of discovery.

omHSSPage.heroText: HÄSSELBY STRANDS SJÖSCOUTKÅR - Hässelby Strands Sjöscoutkår - A traditional non-profit youth organization for many years
omHSSPage.whoDesc1: Hässelby Strands Sjöscoutkår (HSS) is a traditional, non-profit youth organization focusing on scouting and boating. We operate in Hässelby Strand, western Stockholm, and have done so for many years.
omHSSPage.whoDesc2: We are religiously and politically independent and a member of the Swedish Scout Association (Scouterna), which with approximately 65,000 members is one of Sweden's largest youth organizations.
omHSSPage.whoDesc3: With us, children and youth from all parts of society meet to experience adventures together, learn new skills, and grow as individuals.
omHSSPage.uniqueDesc1: As a sea scout troupe, we have a unique focus on water and boats. We sail in Lake Mälaren and the Stockholm archipelago, teaching our scouts everything about maritime safety, navigation, and sailing.
omHSSPage.uniqueDesc2: We have activities during all seasons - from summer sailing to winter hikes. Our scouts get to experience both nature and challenges all year round.
omHSSPage.uniqueDesc3: Here, we create strong friendships that last a lifetime. Many of our leaders are former scouts who continue to be involved in the organization.
omHSSPage.uniqueDesc4: We believe in learning through practical experience. Our scouts get to try, fail, and succeed in a safe environment where mistakes are a part of learning.
omHSSPage.historyDesc1: Hässelby Strands Sjöscoutkår has a long and proud tradition of scouting activities in the area. Over the years, thousands of children and youth have passed through our units and learned everything from knots to leadership.
omHSSPage.historyDesc2: Many of our traditions live on today - from the ice-hole swim in winter to summer sailing in the archipelago. These traditions unite generations of scouts and create a strong sense of belonging.
omHSSPage.historyDesc3: The troupe has always had a strong connection to water and boats. Our location by Lake Mälaren has given us unique opportunities to conduct high-level sea scouting activities.
omHSSPage.traditionDesc1: A classic tradition where Adventurer scouts swim in an ice-hole during the winter. Not only brave - it is also an important exercise in maritime safety!
omHSSPage.traditionDesc2: Every summer we head out on adventures in the Stockholm archipelago. Our scouts learn sailing, navigation, and teamwork.
omHSSPage.traditionDesc3: Adventurer scouts stay in tents even when it is at its coldest. A challenge that strengthens both body and mind!
omHSSPage.partnersSubText: We cooperate with several organizations to provide our scouts with the best possible activities:
omHSSPage.partnerDesc1: We are members of the Swedish Scout Association and follow their guidelines and values.
omHSSPage.partnerDesc2: We are part of the district's community and participate in joint activities.
omHSSPage.partnerDesc3: We cooperate with local associations and organizations in Hässelby Strand to strengthen our operations.

varaBatarPage.heroText: The boats are an important part of our identity, history, and activities. Here you can read about our boats - both those we sail today and those that sailed before us.
varaBatarPage.mainSectionDesc: We believe in learning by doing. Our fleet is adapted to give scouts of all ages the opportunity to learn how to sail, navigate, and handle boats safely in Lake Mälaren and the Stockholm archipelago.
varaBatarPage.fleetDesc: These boats are used in our activities for sailing, training, and adventure on the open water.
varaBatarPage.boatCardDesc1: Perfect for learning the basics of sailing in a safe and fun way.
varaBatarPage.boatCardDesc2: Fast and fun boats well-suited for both training and day cruising.
varaBatarPage.boatCardDesc3: Our largest sailboats. Used for longer voyages, training, and relaxed sailing.
varaBatarPage.boatCardDesc4: Our motorboat, which is always used during sailings and water activities to ensure the highest safety.
varaBatarPage.earlierCardDesc1: Popular Trissjollar dinghies that served as a perfect step between the Optimist and keelboats.
varaBatarPage.earlierCardDesc2: A beautiful red Seacat that was excellent for learning to sail. Renovated by the troupe in 2001.
varaBatarPage.earlierCardDesc3: An appreciated leader boat purchased in 1989. Unfortunately sold after an accident at the shipyard.
varaBatarPage.earlierCardDesc4: A riveted support boat with a lot of character. Unfortunately, it leaked quite a bit towards the end.
varaBatarPage.sailWithUsText: Are you ready to start your scout adventure?

varaPlatserPage.heroText: Ruffen and Myset are our two sea scouting locations. This is where we hold meetings, camps, and adventures all year round.
varaPlatserPage.mainSectionDesc: We have two fantastic locations by Lake Mälaren where we meet, socialize, and create memories.
varaPlatserPage.locationDesc1: Ruffen is located by Lake Mälaren and is our own cabin. Here we have a dock, boat berths, and plenty of space for activities and camps.
varaPlatserPage.locationDesc2: Myset is located by Lake Mälaren and is our second location. (Note: matching original swedish text duplicate structure)
varaPlatserPage.findUsDesc: How to find your way to our locations
varaPlatserPage.parkingDesc: Follow the signs to the gravel parking lot

membersPage.heroText: Here you will find practical information for you as a member or guardian. Information about Scoutnet, Discord, safety, documents, and our routines.
membersPage.accessCardDesc1: We use Discord for communication between older scouts, leaders, and officials.
membersPage.accessCardDesc2: Scoutnet is used for the membership registry, registrations, contact information, and mailings.
membersPage.accessCardDesc3: Here you will find important documents, policies, and information about the troupe.
membersPage.rutinCardDesc1: Notify leaders if you cannot participate in meetings or activities.
membersPage.rutinCardDesc2: Information is primarily sent via Scoutnet and the respective unit's communication channels.
membersPage.rutinCardDesc3: Prior to activities, specific information regarding packing, times, and contact details is always sent out.
membersPage.questionsDesc: Contact your unit leader or reach out to us via the contact page.

footer.adventureDesc: Together we can do more! The Scouts give children and youth from all parts of society the chance to experience adventures together and grow as individuals.
